name: source_reader
description: Read source files with line numbers, search for patterns (function definitions, keywords), or view specific line ranges. Supports batch mode for reading multiple files in a single call using comma-separated file specs with optional line ranges, or glob patterns (e.g. '/path/to/*.go').
when_to_use: Use when you need to examine source code. Replace multiple 'nl', 'sed', 'grep', 'cat', 'head' calls with one script. Use batch mode ('file1:1-50,file2:10-30,...' batch) to read multiple files at once, eliminating sequential cat/read steps. Use glob patterns ('/path/to/*.go' batch) to read all files matching a pattern.

parameters.name: file_path_or_spec
parameters.description: Path to the source file, or comma-separated file specs with optional :line_range suffix (e.g. 'file1.py:10-30,file2.py:40-60') for batch mode, or a glob pattern (e.g. '/path/to/*.go') for batch reading all matching files

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Action: 'grep' (search single pattern), 'lines' (show line range), 'functions' (list def/class lines), 'all' (functions + first 50 lines, default), 'batch' (read multiple file specs at once)

parameters.name: pattern_or_line_start
parameters.description: For 'grep' action: pattern to search (default: 'def '). For 'lines' and 'all' actions: starting line number (default: 1)

parameters.name: line_end
parameters.description: Ending line number (used with lines action, default: 50 or end of file)

examples.expected: All function/method definitions in the file with line numbers.
All lines containing 'contact' with line numbers.
First file fully, second file lines 1-100, all with line numbers in a single output.
First 50 lines of every .go file under /app/internal/module/, with line numbers.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-3 separate tool calls (one to grep for functions, another to read specific lines) with a single script call. Batch mode eliminates 5-10 sequential cat/read steps by reading multiple files at once. Glob pattern support eliminates the need to manually list each file, further reducing round trips. Reduces token usage by combining output into one structured response.
